claims: AC1.1: createPane creates panes in an active tmux session and returns pane IDs confirmed by tmux list-panes.
AC1.2: sendKeys writes shell commands into the target pane and the output appears in pane capture within 2 seconds.
AC1.3: waitForProcessExit resolves only after the running pane process returns to the shell, with the happy-path regression test synchronized on the running process to avoid flakiness.
AC1.4: invalid pane IDs cause sendKeys to reject with TmuxError including the pane ID.

execution_steps.summary: Updated tests/node/us001-tmux-pane-manager.test.mjs AC1.3 happy to assert wait duration against sleep 1 instead of re-checking pane_current_command after resolution.
RED: targeted AC1.3 happy failed because waitForProcessExit returned in about 25 ms, proving the test started waiting before the pane entered sleep.
Applied the minimal fix in the AC1.3 happy test only: wait for pane_current_command to become sleep before starting the elapsed-time assertion; production pane-manager.mjs was not modified.
GREEN: targeted AC1.3 happy passed with about 1.4 s wait time, showing waitForProcessExit resolved after the running sleep process completed.
US-001 AC1.3 subset passed 3/3 and the full US-001 suite passed 12/12 with the updated regression test.

execution_steps.command: for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do node --test tests/node/us001-tmux-pane-manager.test.mjs || exit $?; done
execution_steps.summary: Determinism check passed: 5 consecutive isolated full-suite runs all exited 0 with 12/12 passing.
Canonical L1 verification passed across US-00 and US-001.
